title: Parapolystichum perrierianum
taxonomy: Parapolystichum perrierianum is a species of fern in the family Dryopteridaceae. It is a type of bush fern native to Madagascar. The plant has been classified based on its characteristics and morphology, including its frond and leaf structure. This fern is often found growing in humid environments and is adapted to withstand a range of temperatures. Further classification may involve examining its genetic makeup and phylogenetic relationships with other fern species.

intro: Parapolystichum perrierianum, also known as the Madagascar shield fern, is a species of fern native to Madagascar. Traditionally, this plant has been used in medicine for its various health benefits, including the treatment of fever, inflammation, and gastrointestinal issues.
